page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AWS Graviton processor. Built on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture, this instance features an AWS Neoverse-N1 CPU with 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average to poor performance across various workloads. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yielded average results of 1520.19 and 6055.75, respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block reads at 63.9 GB/sec and writes at 30.56 GB/sec, though small-block cached reads are weaker at 236.33 GB/sec. The instance exhibits poor performance in single-threaded and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, as well as in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests. Passmark database operations perform at an average level of 2648.72, whereas Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput fall into the weaker performance category. LLM inference speeds for a small 135M model are poor, processing prompts at 101.51 tokens/sec and generating text at 24.4 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6gd.xlarge offers a balanced resource profile by combining dedicated ARM-based compute cores, a standard memory-to-vCPU ratio, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage. This hardware configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, and database operations that benefit from local instance storage, though it is less suited for intensive cryptographic, compression, or machine learning inference tasks due to lower benchmark scores in those areas.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of memory, a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Performance benchmarks show average results in stress-ng CPU tests and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside average performance in Passmark database operations. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic tasks, software compilation, and LLM inference. Qualitatively, the server provides a cost-efficient balance of dedicated ARM compute and local high-speed storage. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, multi-threaded processing, and database workloads that utilize local instance storage, while being less optimal for high-performance computing or intensive compression tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server featuring 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD, suitable for database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server with 4 Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM-based general-purpose server featuring dedicated physical cores and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for balanced workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of memory and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Delivers average performance in database operations and stress-ng CPU benchmarks
Features 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for general-purpose applications and database workloads utilizing local storage
